skill.name: backend-development-feature-development
skill.description: Coordinates backend feature delivery from requirements through architecture, implementation, testing, deployment, monitoring, and documentation.
skill.summary: Guides structured backend feature delivery across planning, build, validation, rollout, and operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 17 weak cryptography patterns and one system reconnaissance pattern, but review found no executable cryptography, reconnaissance commands, network calls, or obfuscated behavior. The matches are false positives caused by ordinary workflow text such as description, design, DDD, documentation, and production approval guidance. The skill is safe to publish with low operational caution because it discusses deployment and production rollout planning.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain normal backend workflow language, including description text, DDD methodology, architecture design prompts, documentation guidance, and feature description placeholders. No evidence found of weak hash functions, ciphers, encryption code, key handling, or credential processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown workflow instructions at these locations. Semantic review found no cryptographic implementation, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive secret handling.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged safety guidance warns users to avoid production changes without approvals and rollback plans. No evidence found of host discovery, environment probing, command execution, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a safety recommendation, not an instruction to inspect or enumerate a system. There are no adjacent commands or tooling references that would enable reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Operational Deployment Guidance Requires User Oversight
low_findings.description: The skill includes rollout, feature flag, monitoring, and rollback planning for production delivery. This is legitimate feature-development guidance, but users should treat production changes as human-approved operational work.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The operational impact is clear from deployment and rollback sections, but the skill also includes approval, staging, validation, and rollback safeguards. This supports a low caution rather than a confirmed security issue.

content.value_statement: Complex backend features often fail when planning, implementation, testing, and rollout are handled in isolation. This skill provides a structured workflow for coordinating requirements, architecture, delivery, validation, deployment, and operations.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a backend feature request into discovery, architecture, implementation, testing, deployment, and monitoring phases.
Defines phase outputs such as requirements documents, technical designs, API contracts, test suites, runbooks, and dashboards.
Supports development methods including traditional delivery, TDD, BDD, and domain-driven design.
Plans rollout approaches such as direct release, canary, feature flags, blue-green deployment, and A/B testing.
Includes security validation, performance optimization, documentation, and rollback planning steps.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an executable implementation tool.
It depends on external specialist agents or human teams to produce detailed artifacts.
It does not verify generated code, infrastructure changes, or production readiness by itself.
It is too heavy for small isolated fixes that do not need cross-team coordination.

use_cases.title: Plan a Multi-Service Feature
use_cases.description: Create requirements, architecture, API contracts, risk checks, and rollout steps for a feature that spans several backend services.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Organize testing, security validation, performance goals, observability, documentation, and rollback plans before a controlled production launch.

use_cases.title: Align Product and Engineering Scope
use_cases.description: Turn a business request into user stories, success metrics, delivery phases, dependencies, and clear acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Start Feature Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for an end-to-end delivery plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a backend feature for: [feature description]. Use traditional delivery, medium complexity, and feature-flag rollout. Include requirements, risks, testing, deployment, and documentation outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Service Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on architecture and implementation phases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill for: [feature description]. Focus on service boundaries, API contracts, data models, integration points, resilience patterns, and implementation tasks. Assume TDD and canary deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Controlled Launch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for release readiness and operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare release readiness for: [feature description]. Include security testing, performance budgets, feature flags, observability dashboards, alerts, rollback steps, and approval checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Epic Delivery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill for an epic backend initiative: [initiative description]. Apply domain-driven design, map bounded contexts, coordinate frontend and data dependencies, define phased rollout, and produce team handoff artifacts.

output_examples.input: Plan a subscription billing feature with feature flags and canary rollout.
output: A phased delivery plan covering requirements, technical design, backend implementation, frontend integration, data events, and QA.
A release plan with feature flag setup, canary percentage, monitoring dashboards, rollback steps, and documentation owners.

output_examples.input: Prepare a complex migration-backed feature for production launch.
output: A risk assessment covering migration safety, privacy requirements, dependencies, test coverage, and operational readiness.
A launch checklist with staging validation, performance budgets, alert thresholds, approval gates, and post-launch review tasks.

best_practices: Define success metrics, scope boundaries, and rollback criteria before implementation starts.
Use feature flags and staged validation for changes with production or data impact.
Carry context from each phase into later testing, deployment, monitoring, and documentation work.
anti_patterns: Using the full workflow for a small isolated bug fix.
Skipping security, performance, or observability phases for a production-facing feature.
Treating generated plans as approval to deploy without human review.

faq.question: What kind of work is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for coordinated backend feature delivery that needs planning, implementation, validation, deployment, monitoring, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it write the production code for me?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation tasks, but code quality and production readiness still require review, tests, and environment-specific validation.

faq.question: Which development methods does it support?
faq.answer: It supports traditional delivery, test-driven development, behavior-driven development, and domain-driven design.

faq.question: Does it include deployment planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers direct rollout, canary release, feature flags, blue-green deployment, A/B testing, monitoring, and rollback planning.

faq.question: Is this suitable for small fixes?
faq.answer: No. The workflow is designed for features with multiple phases, dependencies, or operational risk.

faq.question: How should teams use the generated plan?
faq.answer: Teams should review each phase, assign owners, validate assumptions, and require approval before production changes.
